name: SimpleDAO — Reentrancy
annotatedLines: Known reentrancy — withdraw before state update
notes: The most referenced reentrancy example. Mirrors original DAO hack pattern. Should be flagged as Critical reentrancy.
attackAngle: Reentrancy via external call before balance update in withdraw()

name: Reentrancy Insecure
annotatedLines: CEI violation — external call before userBalances reset
notes: Classic CEI violation. R4qib should flag the call before state update pattern.

name: Parity Wallet — Access Control
annotatedLines: Unprotected initWallet() — missing access control on initialisation
notes: The Parity wallet hack. initWallet() callable by anyone. R4qib should flag unprotected initialiser.
attackAngle: Anyone can call initWallet() and become owner

name: Rubixi — Access Control
annotatedLines: Constructor name mismatch — function named DynamicPyramid not Rubixi
notes: Classic constructor name bug. Old Solidity pattern — constructor not matching contract name becomes public function. Interesting Cat 9 candidate too.
attackAngle: Anyone can call DynamicPyramid() and claim ownership

name: Integer Overflow — Arithmetic
notes: Minimal overflow example. Pre-0.8.x contract with no SafeMath. Should be a clean true positive for Cat 2.

name: Unchecked Return Value
annotatedLines: Return value of send() not checked
notes: Minimal unchecked send example. Should be a clean true positive for Cat 4.
attackAngle: send() can fail silently — execution continues regardless

name: King of the Ether — Unchecked
notes: Real historical contract. More complex than minimal examples — good test of false positive discipline.
attackAngle: send() to previous king fails silently if receiver is contract

name: Lottery — Bad Randomness
notes: block.number for randomness — miner manipulable. Maps to Cat 7 front-running in R4qib.
attackAngle: Miner controls block.number — can determine lottery winner

name: Time Manipulation — Roulette
notes: Timestamp dependence for randomness. Miner-manipulable within ~15 second window.
attackAngle: Miner adjusts timestamp to win roulette

name: Denial of Service — Array
notes: Gas exhaustion via unbounded array iteration. Maps to Cat 6 logic errors. Good test of business logic reasoning.
attackAngle: Attacker adds many addresses to creditors — refund() runs out of gas
